brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (top shelf) with valid reasoning grounded in Rachel's own action; goes one level beyond surface by noting Rachel didn't witness Tyler's reversal, but doesn't trace second-order effects like whether Rachel's later re-sorting would update her belief; no explicit reflection on reasoning method or bias check, just linear progression; cites a direct story quote to support the claim but does not test boundary conditions or counterfactuals; acknowledges absence of evidence that Rachel saw the move, but doesn't quantify or structure uncertainty; never considers 'the counter' as a competing location Rachel might check first, nor explains why it was rejected; every conclusion traces to an explicit story event — the chain from Rachel-moved-it to Rachel-would-look-there is fully reproducible
